activity_question: Which WNBA team did Catarina Pollini play for?

title: Slam Dunk Star Born
story: Bouncing into the world on March 16, 1989, Blake Griffin would grow up to become a basketball sensation. This Oklahoma City native's incredible leaping ability and powerful dunks made him a fan favorite. Griffin's talent led him to become a six-time NBA All-Star and win Rookie of the Year in 2011. He's played for teams like the Clippers, Pistons, Nets, and Celtics.

title: Mapping the World's Rocks
story: Ami Boué, a pioneering geologist, was born in Hamburg on March 16, 1794. He grew up to become one of the first people to create a geological map of the entire world! Boué's work helped scientists understand how different rocks and minerals are spread across our planet. His map was like a colorful puzzle showing Earth's hidden treasures.

title: A Surprising Visitor in Plymouth
story: Imagine the colonists' surprise when a Native American chief walked boldly into their settlement! On March 16, 1621, Massasoit, leader of the Wampanoag people, visited Plymouth. This brave move led to a peace treaty between the Native Americans and the English settlers, helping both groups survive in the New World.

title: West Point: America's First Military School
story: Congress made a big decision in 1802. They voted to create the United States Military Academy at West Point. This school would train future army leaders. On March 16, the plan became official. A few months later, on July 4, West Point opened its doors to the first cadets.

title: Scotland Yard: London's New Police Force
story: Criminals beware! In 1830, London got a powerful new weapon against crime. On March 16, the city's police force was completely reorganized. This new group of officers became known as Scotland Yard. They patrolled the streets, keeping people safe and solving mysteries. It was a big change that helped make London a more secure place to live.

title: Breaking Barriers in Medicine
story: Susan Hayhurst shattered glass ceilings in 1883. On March 16, she became the first woman in the United States to graduate from a pharmacy college. Her groundbreaking achievement opened doors for future generations of women in medicine. Hayhurst's determination paved the way for more diverse voices in healthcare, proving that passion and knowledge know no gender.

title: Hockey History: Senators Roar to Victory
story: Skates sliced the ice as the Ottawa Senators faced off against the Port Arthur Bearcats in 1911. The Senators were unstoppable, scoring goal after goal. When the final whistle blew on March 16, the scoreboard showed a whopping 13-4 victory for Ottawa. This thrilling match took place at Dey's Arena, with the Senators claiming the coveted Stanley Cup.
activity_question: Where did this historic hockey game take place?
bonus_fact: The Stanley Cup is the oldest trophy in North American professional sports.

title: Fierce Blizzard Strikes the Midwest
story: Howling winds and blinding snow swept across North Dakota and Minnesota on March 16, 1941. This powerful blizzard caught many by surprise, trapping people in cars and homes. Despite the danger, brave neighbors and rescue teams worked tirelessly to help those in need. The storm taught important lessons about weather preparedness and looking out for one another during tough times.

title: Pirate Radio's Unexpected Adventure
story: Ahoy, matey! In 1967, a daring radio station called Pirate Radio 333 sailed the seas, broadcasting tunes from a ship. But on March 16, their musical voyage hit a snag.
